article_title: FTC Opens Industrywide Antitrust Probe Into Fertilizer Prices as Ferguson Faces Farmers From 18 States in Texas
summary: Every quote verbatim and every specific traced to a cited non-bot-blocked source; bot-blocked market-share figures correctly dropped. Only issue is a non-blocking allowlist warning — no factual corrections needed.

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News piece (774 words) with clear Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know / Analysis sections. Each claim is inline-cited to a specific outlet. Appropriately separates confirmed facts from open questions.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read all 5 gzipped snapshots from sources/2026-06/ftc-opens-industrywide-antitrust-probe-into-fertilizer-prices-as-ferguson-faces-farmers-from-18-states-in-texas/ (all HTTP 200, no archive fallbacks). source-0 (DTN/Progressive Farmer): confirmed compulsory-process quote, 'Grayson County, Texas, about an hour north of the Dallas metro area', 'More than 100 corn farmers from as many as 18 states', 'substantial majority'/'few corporations', 'little agency'/'$1 billion'/'10 (law) firms', 'judge and jury'/'evidence on the ground', 'continue to ignore' — all verbatim; URL path encodes 2026/05/28 confirming the May 28 2026 date. source-1 (PYMNTS): confirmed 'on my order...precipitous rise...everyone in this room', 'USDA data has shown the single largest increase...since 2020 has come from fertilizer', named firms (Mosaic, CF Industries, Nutrien, Koch, Norway-based Yara International) 'did not immediately respond', and DOJ 'both civil and criminal investigations into possible price collusion involving several leading commercial fertilizer manufacturers' — all verbatim. source-2 (Farm Policy News): confirmed all YoY percentages verbatim (anhydrous 44%, UAN28 28%, urea 27%, MAP 15%, DAP 14%, potash 5%). source-3 (Illinois Corn): confirmed '150% since 2020', 'net farm income fell 31% from its 2022 peak', Bunselmeyer quote verbatim, 'Fed Up: Fertilizer Cartel Profits off Farmers' Backs and Your Grocery Bill' event title verbatim, May 28 2026 North Texas, 18 states. source-4 (RFD-TV): confirmed Texas Corn Producers Association 'blamed concentrated market power for higher prices'.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All specifics, quotes, names, and dates trace to cited non-bot-blocked snapshots. CRITICAL CHECK PASSED: the market-share figures (80% phosphorus / 90% potash / 70% nitrogen) that only appeared in bot-blocked search snippets (AgWeb, Michigan Farm News) are genuinely ABSENT from the article — replaced by Ferguson's verbatim qualitative framing ('substantial majority' / 'few corporations'). No fabricated docket or executive-order numbers anywhere. 'potash' appears in the body only within the legitimately cited YoY list (potash 5% higher). Minor inter-source geographic discrepancy noted (DTN: Grayson County / hour north of Dallas; RFD-TV: McKinney) — the article uses DTN's attribution correctly; not an error.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism, no editorializing, no AI self-reference. The provocative 'Fed Up: Fertilizer Cartel' phrasing is correctly quoted and attributed as the event's own billed title, not adopted as the article's voice.
editor_notes.originality: New topic. No prior fertilizer or FTC-fertilizer-probe coverage in the archive; existing FTC/antitrust articles cover unrelated matters (Rollins noncompete, EU DMA, Meta/WhatsApp, Google).
recommendations: Sources dtnpf.com, farmpolicynews.illinois.edu, ilcorn.org, and rfdtv.com are not in config/source_allowlist.txt. This is a non-blocking warning; all four are reputable agricultural-trade and university-extension outlets and verified against snapshots. A maintainer may consider adding them to the allowlist.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, accurate, well-sourced submission. Every quote is verbatim with correct attribution, every number and name traces to a cited source, and the agent correctly dropped the bot-blocked market-share figures. The sole finding is a non-blocking allowlist warning that does not require a corrections record. Verdict overridden from the script's APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS to APPROVE — the script's downgrade was driven solely by the allowlist warning, which per editorial policy is non-blocking and not a factual correction.

content_preview.title: FTC Opens Industrywide Antitrust Probe Into Fertilizer Prices as Ferguson Faces Farmers From 18 States in Texas
content_preview.summary: FTC Chair Andrew Ferguson told corn farmers in Texas the agency has opened a compulsory-process antitrust investigation into surging fertilizer prices.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The Federal Trade Commission has opened a broad antitrust investigation into the rising cost of fertilizer, FTC Chairman Andrew Ferguson announced during an agricultural event in Texas, according to [PYMNTS](https://www.pymnts.com/cpi-posts/ftc-opens-broad-antitrust-probe-into-fertilizer-price-surge-affecting-us-farmers/). Speaking to corn growers, Ferguson said the agency's probe is already underway: "I'm announcing that, on my order, the commission some time ago commenced a major ...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains (dtnpf.com, farmpolicynews.illinois.edu, ilcorn.org, rfdtv.com) to config/source_allowlist.txt — non-blocking.
